title: Capstone 12 — Video Understanding Pipeline (Scene, QA, Search)

questions.question: Which three vector types does each scene get in the multi-vector index?
options: Caption embedding, keyframe embedding, and transcript embedding
questions.explanation: Scene segmentation uses CLIP embeddings to detect shot boundaries and keyframes, dividing long videos into semantically coherent segments for processing.

questions.question: How are results from the three retrieval streams merged at query time?
options: By picking the caption hit only
Via reciprocal rank fusion across the three ranked lists
By taking the union without scoring
questions.explanation: Temporal modeling uses VideoMAE or similar architectures to understand motion patterns and temporal relationships between frames across video segments.

questions.question: What does the temporal grounding step refine inside the top scene?
options: The transcript word timestamps from Whisper
The number of vectors stored in Qdrant
The (start, end) timestamp window that contains the answer
questions.explanation: Multi-modal QA combines visual features (frame embeddings), audio features (speech transcription, sound analysis), and text features (captions, subtitles) for comprehensive understanding.

questions.question: Which question class is reported separately because it is a known hallucination hotspot?
options: Counting and action-type questions, where VLMs miscount or mis-order events
questions.explanation: Search indexing stores segment embeddings with metadata (timestamps, visual tags, audio transcripts), enabling temporal search (finding events at specific times) and semantic search (finding similar scenes).

questions.question: Which grounding metric does the rubric measure on a held-out set?
questions.explanation: The capstone measures scene segmentation accuracy (boundary detection F1 score), QA accuracy (correctness of answers to video questions), and search relevance (retrieval quality for video queries).

questions.question: Why does the pipeline require cited timestamps in answers?
options: Timestamps are required by Whisper output
Timestamps are required by Qdrant payloads
They let the viewer jump to the exact (video_id, start, end) so users can verify the claim
questions.explanation: Processing efficiency is measured by throughput (frames per second) and latency (time to process a 10-minute video end-to-end).
